headline: Microsoft's decade-old Secure Boot certificates begin expiring June 24, threatening the trusted boot chain on Windows and Linux machines that skip the update.

emerging_trends.text: Threats are sinking below the software layer into silicon itself, with the HAMLOCK backdoor hiding inside the FPGAs and ASICs that run AI models on edge devices, where no patch can reach.
Aging cryptographic trust anchors are becoming a liability as the original Secure Boot certificates expire on June 24, leaving unpatched machines unable to verify their own boot loaders.
Extortion crews keep turning stolen corporate troves into public leverage, the way the Madison Square Garden attackers now claim to have dumped their haul.

topics.title: Secure Boot Certificate Expiration
topics.latest_developments: Wired warned that the Microsoft-issued cryptographic keys underpinning UEFI Secure Boot start expiring June 24, 2026, on Windows and Linux machines.
topics.summary: Microsoft issued the certificates that validate a computer's boot sequence in 2011, and they now reach end of life. Machines that miss the firmware and certificate refresh can no longer trust new boot-loader updates, opening a path for bootkits, so users and administrators should apply vendor Secure Boot updates before the deadline.

sources.title: A Critical Deadline Is Approaching for Windows and Linux Security

topics.title: HAMLOCK Hardware Neural-Network Backdoor
topics.latest_developments: Researchers at the University of Tennessee and the University of Florida unveiled HAMLOCK, a backdoor that hides inside the FPGAs and ASICs running deep-learning models on edge devices by splitting its malicious logic across the hardware.
topics.summary: Edge AI systems lean on third-party accelerator chips for speed, which creates supply-chain exposure. HAMLOCK spreads its malicious functionality across that silicon so it evades inspection and triggers on demand, putting any organization that buys rather than builds its AI hardware at risk.

sources.title: Week in review: 74k Fortinet firewall credentials stolen, Splunk Enterprise RCE under active attack

topics.title: Madison Square Garden Data Leak
topics.latest_developments: Wired reported that the attackers behind the Madison Square Garden breach now claim to have leaked the stolen data publicly.
topics.summary: The trove tied to Madison Square Garden Entertainment includes a risk-rated list of celebrities and players alongside New York Knicks records. People named in the data face heightened targeting risk.

sources.title: Hackers Claim to Leak Stolen Madison Square Garden Data

weather: Tonight: Mostly Cloudy then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, low 62F.
Monday: Showers And Thunderstorms then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 77F.

games.recap: McCarthy hits leadoff inside-the-park homer off Skenes in Rockies' 2-1 win over Pirates

games.result: Pirates 0 · Rockies 1 · Top 4th (in progress at last update)

business_politics.title: Iran Pauses U.S. Talks After Trump Strike Threat
business_politics.latest_developments: Tehran said Sunday it paused the Switzerland negotiations after Trump threatened to strike Iran over its backing of Hezbollah, even as Vice President JD Vance met Iranian officials near Lake Lucerne to launch the talks.
business_politics.summary: U.S. and Iranian negotiators gathered at a Swiss resort to build out last week's interim deal ending the war, with Vance meeting parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf and Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi; Trump's threat over the Israel-Hezbollah fighting in Lebanon now clouds whether the talks survive.

sources.title: Iran Says It Is Pausing Talks After Trump Threats
US and Iran hold crunch talks in Switzerland

business_politics.title: Starmer on Brink of Resigning as UK Prime Minister
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump declared Sunday that Keir Starmer "will resign," and the prime minister went dark at Chequers to weigh his departure with his wife, Victoria, with some ministers expecting an announcement next week.
business_politics.summary: Andy Burnham's by-election win has pushed Starmer toward quitting as British prime minister, and investors watch whether a Labour leadership change pressures UK gilts as Burnham's team already explores government appointments.

sources.title: Starmer on brink of quitting as UK prime minister
Starmer could announce exit next week, some ministers believe

business.title: Galaxie Electronics Closes in Squirrel Hill
business.latest_developments: Galaxie Electronics shut its doors after 30 years selling and repairing turntables in Squirrel Hill.
business.summary: Galaxie Electronics, the Squirrel Hill shop known for "keeping the tables turning," closed permanently after three decades in business, ending a longtime Pittsburgh source for audio gear and repairs.

sources.title: Galaxie Electronics closes after 30 years in Pittsburgh

business.title: Penn State Fayette Campus Feasibility Study
business.latest_developments: Fayette County leaders and community partners launched a comprehensive feasibility study to weigh future uses for the Penn State Fayette campus property.
business.summary: The study will evaluate future opportunities for the Penn State Fayette campus property, as Fayette County officials look to chart what comes next for the site.

sources.title: Feasibility study to explore future possibilities for Penn State Fayette campus

around_town.title: North Huntingdon Plans Irwin-Larimer Trail Study
around_town.latest_developments: North Huntingdon officials approved hiring a consultant to study a walking and biking trail linking downtown Irwin to the township's Larimer village.
around_town.summary: The feasibility study will assess a proposed multiuse trail connecting downtown Irwin to the North Huntingdon community of Larimer in Westmoreland County.

sources.title: North Huntingdon seeks proposals for trail study

around_town.title: Hempfield Senior Tax Rebate Checks Coming
around_town.latest_developments: Hempfield reported more applicants than expected for its new senior tax rebate program, and approved seniors should receive checks within the next few months.
around_town.summary: Hempfield Township's new senior tax rebate program drew heavier-than-anticipated demand, with approved applicants set to be reimbursed in the coming months.

sources.title: Applicants for Hempfield tax rebate 'more than expected'

around_teams.title: Brandon Lowe on Fatherhood After Infertility
around_teams.latest_developments: In a Post-Gazette Father's Day feature, Pirates infielder Brandon Lowe called fatherhood "the greatest gift" after he and his wife battled infertility and turned to IVF.
around_teams.summary: The Post-Gazette profiled Pirates infielder Brandon Lowe, who spoke about his and his wife's struggle with infertility and their path through IVF to having children.

sources.title: After a battle with infertility, the Pirates' Brandon Lowe sees fatherhood as 'the greatest gift'

team_usa.title: USMNT's Alex Freeman and His NFL Roots
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN profiled U.S. men's national team defender Alex Freeman, son of former Green Bay Packers Pro Bowl receiver Antonio Freeman, on how his family steered him to the 2026 World Cup.
team_usa.summary: The feature traces how Antonio Freeman taught his son Alex what it takes to be a professional, while Alex's mother and stepfather gave him his love of soccer, a path that landed him on the USMNT defense for the home World Cup.

sources.title: How Alex Freeman's Pro Bowl dad helped him live his World Cup dream

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 2)
reading.summary: Building on his scoop that OpenAI spent $34 billion to make $13.07 billion in revenue, Zitron argues that years of inflated AI valuations have created a Silicon Valley bubble now approaching its end.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Michael Morton on how AI reshapes e-commerce, covering unfalsifiable bear cases, distribution versus referral models, grocery, and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Responding to a Financial Times interview with Rebecca Hinds of the Work AI Institute about a survey of 6,000 digital workers, Newport argues that AI exposes dysfunction in knowledge work that long predates the technology.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
